phases.name: Lift Report columns onto a typed discriminated-union kernel so malformed columns are rejected at the boundary

steps.name: Reject a column with an unknown semantic role
steps.criteria: Submitting a report whose column carries an unknown semantic_role via create_report returns a structured Failure/422 at the boundary and persists nothing.
steps.scenario_name: A report column with an unknown semantic role is rejected at the boundary

steps.name: Reject an illegal role/type pairing via the typed union
steps.criteria: A report column with an illegal role/type pairing is rejected by the Pydantic discriminated union (not validate_columns_metadata), returning a structured error and persisting nothing.
steps.scenario_name: A report column with an illegal role and type pairing is rejected by the typed union

steps.name: Load every existing report through the typed kernel
steps.criteria: Every existing report payload hydrates through the typed ProjectionColumn/Measure kernel without error (production-shape coverage).
steps.scenario_name: Every existing report loads through the typed projection kernel

phases.name: Consolidate the View/Report compilers into one kernel visitor + report extension with a build-time completeness check

steps.name: Render the same SQL as the separate compilers
steps.criteria: For an in-test view/report, the consolidated kernel visitor + report extension renders the same DuckDB SQL as the separate ViewIbisCompiler/ReportIbisCompiler (self-contained pre-vs-post equivalence, no legacy snapshot).
steps.scenario_name: The consolidated renderer produces the same SQL as the pre-consolidation compilers

steps.name: Fail the build on an unhandled component discriminator
steps.criteria: A component discriminator with no entry in an active visitor fails at build/collection time (completeness check), not a silent skip.
steps.scenario_name: An unhandled component discriminator fails the build instead of silently skipping

steps.name: Render an entity-only report through the shared kernel path
steps.criteria: An entity-only report renders as the kernel visitor's output with no aggregation step (shared path, not a special-cased branch).
steps.scenario_name: An entity-only report renders through the shared kernel path with no aggregation step

phases.name: Disaggregate filters into the shared relation_filters table, establishing the component-table repository + polymorphic cascade

steps.name: Write exactly one filter row when adding a filter
steps.criteria: Adding one filter via the view update path writes exactly one relation_filters row (no whole-array rewrite).
steps.scenario_name: Adding one filter writes exactly one filter row

steps.name: Backfill every embedded filter into one keyed row
steps.criteria: The expand migration backfills each embedded filters JSON element into one relation_filters row keyed by (parent_type, parent_id); the renderer reads filters from rows.
steps.scenario_name: The backfill turns every embedded filter into one keyed row

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged when filters are reordered
steps.criteria: Reordering two filters leaves the rendered SQL unchanged (filters commutative; no order column).
steps.scenario_name: Reordering two filters leaves the rendered SQL unchanged

steps.name: Cascade-delete only the parent's own filter rows
steps.criteria: Deleting a parent relation removes only its own relation_filters rows and leaves other relations' rows intact (polymorphic cascade).
steps.scenario_name: Deleting a relation removes only its own filter rows

steps.name: Scope filter loads to the relation's tenant
steps.criteria: relation_filters rows carry an indexed org_id and loads are org_id-scoped.
steps.scenario_name: Loading a relation's filters is scoped to its tenant

phases.name: Move view and report projected columns into one shared relation_columns table

steps.name: List views and reports projecting a shared column
steps.criteria: One query over relation_columns lists every view and report projecting a given output column (shared cross-role projection over ColumnRole).
steps.scenario_name: One query lists every view and report projecting the same column

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged when columns are reordered
steps.criteria: Reordering columns leaves the rendered SQL unchanged (position is presentation-only).
steps.scenario_name: Reordering columns leaves the rendered SQL unchanged

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged when position changes
steps.criteria: Changing a column's presentation position leaves the rendered SQL unchanged.
steps.scenario_name: Changing a column's presentation position leaves the rendered SQL unchanged

steps.name: Write exactly one column row when adding a column
steps.criteria: Adding one column writes exactly one relation_columns row (single-row insert).
steps.scenario_name: Adding one column writes exactly one column row

phases.name: Normalize joins with an explicit declaration-ordered sequence, preserving compiled SQL

steps.name: Produce different SQL when two joins' sequence is swapped
steps.criteria: Swapping two relation_joins.sequence values changes the rendered JOIN order (SQL differs).
steps.scenario_name: Swapping two joins' sequence produces different SQL

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged for declaration-ordered joins
steps.criteria: Joins read in sequence order render the same SQL as the equivalent embedded-array view built in the test (self-contained equivalence).
steps.scenario_name: Joins read in declaration order keep the rendered SQL byte-identical

steps.name: Assign join sequence from array position in the backfill
steps.criteria: The expand migration assigns join sequence by array position (ROW_NUMBER over the array index), not created_at.
steps.scenario_name: The backfill assigns join sequence from array position not creation time

phases.name: Store a relation's grain as queryable rows, one row per parent

steps.name: Query a relation's grain keys as rows
steps.criteria: A relation's grain keys are queryable as relation_grain rows.
steps.scenario_name: A relation's grain keys are queryable as rows

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged when grain keys are reordered
steps.criteria: Reordering grain keys leaves the rendered SQL unchanged (set-like; no order column).
steps.scenario_name: Reordering grain keys leaves the rendered SQL unchanged

steps.name: Produce exactly one grain row per relation
steps.criteria: The backfill produces exactly one relation_grain row per relation (1:1 with ViewGrain, per OQ-3).
steps.scenario_name: The backfill produces exactly one grain row per relation

phases.name: Add the report-only relation_aggregations table and enforce report rules over typed rows

steps.name: Reject a measure with no dimension over typed rows
steps.criteria: A report with a measure and no dimension is rejected over typed rows (ReportRequiresDimension), returning a structured 422.
steps.scenario_name: A report with a measure and no dimension is rejected over typed rows

steps.name: Reject a report sourcing another report
steps.criteria: A report whose source_refs point at another report is rejected by the shared composition service (InvalidReportReference).
steps.scenario_name: A report sourcing another report is rejected by the composition service

steps.name: Write exactly one aggregation row for a valid measure
steps.criteria: Binding a valid measure writes exactly one relation_aggregations row (report-only) binding the measure to its aggregation function.
steps.scenario_name: Binding a valid measure writes exactly one aggregation row

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged when aggregations are reordered
steps.criteria: Reordering aggregations leaves the rendered SQL unchanged (independent aggregates).
steps.scenario_name: Reordering aggregations leaves the rendered SQL unchanged

phases.name: Drop the embedded-JSON columns after one safe release, leaving one source of truth

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged after dropping the JSON columns
steps.criteria: After one safe release, the contract migration drops views.{columns,joins,filters,grain} and reports.columns_metadata; rendering an in-test fixture produces SQL unchanged from before the drop (proves nothing still reads JSON).
steps.scenario_name: Dropping the embedded component columns keeps the rendered SQL unchanged

steps.name: Provide a rollback that re-adds and re-backfills the columns
steps.criteria: The contract migration provides a rollback path that re-adds the dropped columns and re-backfills them from the normalized rows.
steps.scenario_name: The contract migration has a rollback path that re-adds and re-backfills the columns
